expectations.text: Uses workflow.fit_offline() or workflow.fit_disk() (not fit_online, since no simulator is available)
expectations.evidence: Line 174: `history = workflow.fit_offline(` — uses fit_offline with train_data dict.

expectations.text: Splits the simulation bank into training and held-out validation/test sets
expectations.evidence: Lines 37-39 define N_TEST=500, N_VAL=500, N_TRAIN=19000. Lines 104-120 perform the split via random permutation into train_data, val_data, test_data dicts.

expectations.text: Does NOT attempt to create or call a simulator function (it's unavailable)
expectations.evidence: No simulator function is defined or called anywhere in the script. Data is loaded from .npz files (lines 53-72). Lines 259-268 explicitly note the simulator is unavailable.

expectations.text: Uses the naming shorthand (inference_variables=, summary_variables=) since no adapter transforms are needed, OR uses an explicit adapter — either is acceptable
expectations.evidence: Lines 131-137: Uses an explicit `bf.Adapter()` chain with .as_set, .convert_dtype, and .concatenate into summary_variables and inference_variables.

expectations.text: Routes observables through summary_variables with a summary network (not inference_conditions)
expectations.evidence: Line 135: `.concatenate(["observables"], into="summary_variables")`. Line 144: `summary_net = bf.networks.SetTransformer(...)`. Line 163: `summary_network=summary_net` passed to BasicWorkflow.

expectations.text: Uses a SetTransformer for the i.i.d. observations
expectations.evidence: Lines 144-150: `summary_net = bf.networks.SetTransformer(summary_dim=SUMMARY_DIM, embed_dims=(32, 32), num_heads=(2, 2), ...)`. Also line 132: `.as_set(["observables"])` correctly reshapes for the SetTransformer.

expectations.text: Passes validation_data= to fit_offline or fit_disk

expectations.evidence: Lines 183-186: Saves history to JSON file. Lines 191-199: Calls `inspect_history(history.history)` and prints the training convergence report, checking for issues.

expectations.text: Uses the held-out test set for compute_default_diagnostics (not the training data)
expectations.evidence: Line 218: `metrics = workflow.compute_default_diagnostics(test_data=test_data, ...)` — uses test_data (lines 117-120), which is built from test_idx (line 107), completely disjoint from training indices.

expectations.text: Checks diagnostics against house thresholds before declaring success
expectations.evidence: Lines 229-242: Calls `check_diagnostics(metrics)` and checks `diag_report["overall"]["decision"]` — raises RuntimeError on STOP, prints warning on WARN, declares pass otherwise. Analysis notes (lines 54-58) document the specific thresholds.

expectations.text: Notes the limitation that posterior predictive checks require access to the simulator, which is unavailable
expectations.evidence: Lines 259-268 in the code: comments and print statements explicitly state PPCs are not possible without the original simulator. Analysis notes line 47: "No PPCs possible. The simulator is unavailable, so we cannot re-simulate from posterior draws."
